title: [v4.4.1-rhel] Partially revert f8bca0f
body:
The Fedora-37 CI VMs used prior to f8bca0f closely matched RHEL-8.8
which is the intended destination of this v4.4.1-rhel release branch.
Importantly this change, along with one or more future commits
(53a8ef8..b9110a1) lead to downstream build failures on RHEL 8.8,
and reproduce using the original Fedora-37 CI VMs. In other words,
leaving the F37 CI VMs in place would have allowed these failures
to be caught during upstream rather than downstream testing.

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
Replace golang.org/x/crypto with forked and patched version to address CVE-2025-22869
```

comments.body: CI is red, build errors in the crypto package - potentially related to our CVE backports?

> CI is red, build errors in the crypto package - potentially related to our CVE backports?

Yes these failures are expected, CI changes resulted in a false-positive build result.  See [the other PR](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/25624), and I plan on opening at least one more.

We're working on fixes to crypto that we'll bring in here and that should make CI green again.  I won't get into the politics here, other than to say "things are happening" to try and re-fix the problem.
@dashea PTAL make sure I spelled and versioned everything correctly.
